reasoning: This document exhibits multiple major structural and visual defects that compromise its utility as a technical guide. The LLM fidelity signals indicate significant issues with reading order, fragmented lists, and the loss of essential UI imagery (replaced by placeholder rectangles). Given the 0.675 composite score and the 'needs_retry' pipeline status, the document requires manual intervention to restore instructional integrity.

concerns.analysis: On Page 5 ('Delete MFA method:'), the source PDF contains a prominent 'Note' box at the top of the page, intended to be read before the steps. In the output JSON, this note is placed at the very end of the section, after all instructions. This reordering significantly changes the logical flow and context of the instructions.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Note: You cannot delete a MFA method if it is set as default. If you only have one MFA method, you will have to add a new one and set it as default to be able to delete the old method
concerns.output_quote: Note: You cannot delete a MFA method if it is set as default. If you only have one MFA method, you will have to add a new one and set it as default to be able to delete the old method

concerns.analysis: Throughout the document, numbered lists (1-6, 1-9, etc.) are pervasive and systematically fragmented. Every time an image or button occurs within a list sequence, the list structure is broken into a new, separate list object. This results in numerous single-item lists and the loss of correct sequence/numbering semantics (e.g., Step 4 becoming item 1 of a new list).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1. Login... 2. Click... 3. Click... [Image] 4. Select... [Image] 5. Verify... [Image] 6. Enter...
concerns.output_quote: The 'Email' section is broken into four separate 'list' objects (one with 3 items, and three with 1 item each), separated by 'image' elements.

concerns.analysis: In the 'Text Message' section of the first page, the visual organization and flow of information is significantly broken. The sequence of buttons does not match the instructions: step 3 should be 'ADD ENROLLMENT' but shows 'NEXT', and step 4 should be 'ADD' but shows 'SUBMIT'. Subsequent steps are followed by large green placeholder boxes. This rearrangement of content blocks and broken visual sequence disrupts the user's ability to follow the instructions correctly.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1, Text Message section: Step 3 has 'ADD ENROLLMENT' button; Step 4 has 'ADD' button; Step 5 has 'NEXT' button; Step 6 has 'SUBMIT' button.
concerns.output_quote: HTML output, Text Message section: Step 3 is followed by 'NEXT' button; Step 4 is followed by 'SUBMIT' button; Steps 5 and 6 are followed by large green rectangles.
concerns.location: How to enroll for MFA: Text Message section

concerns.analysis: Numerous meaningful images, icons, and buttons are missing or broken in the HTML output. Page 2 shows three large green rectangles instead of the Authenticator logo, QR scanning diagram, and success dialog. Page 3 is missing the pencil edit icons next to 'email' and 'phone'. Page 4 is missing the radio button ('Set as default') and checkbox ('Make as default') images. Page 5 is missing the trash can icon. Additionally, nearly all action buttons (SEND OTP, VERIFY, REMOVE, SUBMIT) from pages 3 thr
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Meaningful icons and images on pages 2 (Simeio logo, QR scanning, Success box), 3 (pencil icon), 4 (radio button, checkbox), and 5 (trash icon), plus various action buttons throughout.
concerns.output_quote: HTML output shows green boxes for images on page 2 and entirely omits the icons and buttons mentioned above in the corresponding sections.
concerns.location: Sections for Authenticator (Page 2), Modify MFA (Page 3), Change default (Page 4), and Delete MFA (Page 5)

concerns.analysis: The URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' appears multiple times across all instructions in the source PDF. In the output JSON, these are converted only as plain text runs (with underlined styling) but are not logically represented as clickable links (missing URL/link metadata), failing the accessibility requirement for these to be clickable.
concerns.location: Throughout the document in step 1 of various lists

action_items: Reorder sections to place 'Note' boxes before the relevant instructions.
Repair list structures to ensure continuous numbering (e.g., Step 4 should follow Step 3 even with an intervening image).
Restore missing UI imagery and icons (trash can, pencil, radio buttons).
Convert plain text URLs into functional HTML links.
